title: Daily Routines
shortSummary: Describe your day from morning to night using everyday verbs and time words.
culturalNote: Daily rhythms in China often include an afternoon rest (午休), and many workplaces and schools pause for lunch and a short nap. Morning exercise is a beloved habit, with parks filling up early for tai chi, dancing, and walking. Meals anchor the day, and 'have you eaten?' doubles as a greeting precisely because food and routine are so closely tied together.

keyPhrases.english: I finish work and go home
I go to sleep at eleven

grammar.explanation: Clock times and time words come before the verb: 我七点起床 (I get up at seven). The time can sit right after the subject.

examples.en: I go to work at eight.

grammar.commonMistake: Putting the time after the verb like English: it is 我七点起床, not 我起床七点.

grammar.explanation: Use 先 (first) ... 然后 (then) to order steps: 我先吃饭，然后睡觉 (I eat first, then sleep). 然后 links the next action.

examples.en: Get up and then eat breakfast.

grammar.commonMistake: Using 然后 to mean 'and' between nouns. It links actions in sequence, not items.

dialogue.setup: Two friends, Jia and Ming, compare their daily routines over tea.

lines.speaker: 佳
lines.english: What time do you get up every day?

lines.speaker: 明
lines.pinyin: Wǒ liù diǎn bàn qǐchuáng. Nǐ ne?
lines.english: I get up at half past six. And you?

lines.speaker: 佳
lines.pinyin: Wǒ qī diǎn. Ránhòu wǒ qù shàngbān.
lines.english: Seven. Then I go to work.

lines.speaker: 明
lines.english: What do you do in the evening?

lines.speaker: 佳
lines.pinyin: Wǒ zuò wǎnfàn, ránhòu kàn diànshì.
lines.english: I cook dinner, then watch TV.

lines.speaker: 明
lines.english: I often go running in the evening.

quiz.question: How do you say 'I eat breakfast'?
我先吃饭，_____睡觉。 (then)
quiz.explanation: 然后 links the next action in a sequence.

quiz.question: Which sentence has correct word order?
quiz.explanation: Time (七点) comes before the verb (起床).

quiz.question: Arrange: 回家 / 我 / 下班
quiz.explanation: Subject 我, then 下班 (finish work) and 回家 (go home) in sequence.
